CellAttribute.name: CellAttribute
CellAttribute.brief: The concept CellAttribute represents a non void attribute associated with a cell of a generic map. It can keep a descriptor to one dart of its associated cell, and can contain any information.

members.name: Dart_descriptor
members.brief: Dart descriptor type.

members.name: Dart_const_descriptor
members.brief: Dart const descriptor type.

members.name: Info
members.brief: Type of the information contained in the attribute.
members.detailed: If void , the cell attribute does not have any information.

members.name: Supports_cell_dart
members.brief: Equals to Tag_true to enable the storage of a Dart_descriptor of the associated cell, Tag_false otherwise.

members.name: On_merge
members.brief: Functor called before merging two attributes.
members.detailed: It must be a binary functor taking as argument two references to a model of CellAttribute .

members.name: On_split
members.brief: Functor called after an attribute was split in two.
members.detailed: It must be a binary functor taking as argument two references to a model of CellAttribute .

members.name: Cell_attribute
members.brief: Constructor initializing the information of this attribute by the copy constructor Info(info) .
members.detailed: Defined only if Info is different from void .

members.name: dart
members.brief: Returns one dart of the cell associated to this attribute, if Supports_cell_dart is equal to Tag_true .

members.name: dart
members.brief: Returns one dart of the cell associated to this attribute, when it is const, if Supports_cell_dart is equal to Tag_true .

members.name: set_dart
members.brief: Sets the dart of the cell associated to this attribute to ah , if Supports_cell_dart is equal to Tag_true .
members.detailed: Otherwise, this method does nothing. d belongs to the cell associated to this attribute.

members.name: info
members.brief: Returns the information of this attribute.
members.detailed: Defined only if Info is different from void .

members.name: info
members.brief: Returns the information of this attribute, when it is const.
members.detailed: Defined only if Info is different from void .

CGAL::Cell_attribute.name: CGAL::Cell_attribute
CGAL::Cell_attribute.brief: The class Cell_attribute represents an attribute containing (or not) an information.
CGAL::Cell_attribute.detailed: Is model of CellAttribute Map a model of the GenericMap concept. Info_ the type of the information contained in the attribute. Equal to void by default. Tag is ::Tag_true to enable the storage of a Dart_descriptor of the associated cell, ::Tag_false otherwise. Equal to ::Tag_true by default. OnMerge the type of the functor called before two attributes are merged. Equal to Null_functor by default. OnSplit the type of the functor called after one attribute is split in two. Equal to Null_functor by default. CGAL::Combinatorial_map <d,Items,Alloc> CGAL::Generalized_map<d,Items,Alloc>

CGAL::Cell_attribute_with_id.brief: The class Cell_attribute_with_id represents an attribute containing (or not) an information, and having an id.
CGAL::Cell_attribute_with_id.detailed: Is model of CellAttribute Map a model of the GenericMap concept. Info_ the type of the information contained in the attribute. Equal to void by default. Tag is ::Tag_true to enable the storage of a Dart_descriptor of the associated cell, ::Tag_false otherwise. Equal to ::Tag_true by default. OnMerge the type of the functor called before two attributes are merged. Equal to Null_functor by default. OnSplit the type of the functor called after one attribute is split in two. Equal to Null_functor by default. CGAL::Combinatorial_map <d,Items,Alloc> CGAL::Generalized_map<d,Items,Alloc>

CGAL::Combinatorial_map.name: CGAL::Combinatorial_map
CGAL::Combinatorial_map.brief: The class Combinatorial_map represents a d D combinatorial map.
CGAL::Combinatorial_map.detailed: Two versions exist: one where Darts and non void attributes are stored in memory using Compact_container , using Alloc as allocator, and use handles as descriptors; a second one where Darts and non void attributes are stored in an internal std::vector like data-structure, and use indices as descriptors. The choice between the two versions is done through the item class. Is model of CombinatorialMap d the dimension of the map. Items a model of the GenericMapItems concept. Equal to Generic_map_min_items by default. Alloc has to match the standard allocator requirements. The rebind mechanism Alloc will be used to create appropriate allocators internally with value type Dart . Equal to CGAL_ALLOCATOR(int) from <CGAL/memory.h> by default. Complexity The complexity of sew and unsew is in O ( $ | $ S $ | $ $ \times$ $ | $ c $ | $ ), S being the set of darts of the orbit $ \langle{}$ $ \beta_1$ , $ \ldots$ , $ \beta_{i-2}$ , $ \beta_{i+2}$ , $ \ldots$ , $ \beta_d$ $ \rangle{}$ for the considered dart, and c the biggest j -cell merged or split during the sew such that j -attributes are non void. The complexity of is_sewable is in O ( $ | $ S $ | $ ). The complexity of set_attribute is in O ( $ | $ c $ | $ ), c being the i -cell containing the considered dart. The complexity of is_without_boundary(i) is in O ( $ | $ D $ | $ ), D being the set of darts of the combinatorial map, and the complexity of is_without_boundary() is in O ( $ | $ D $ | $ $ \times$ d ). The complexity of unmark_all and free_mark is in O ( 1 ) if all the darts of the combinatorial map have the same mark, and in O ( $ | $ D $ | $ ) otherwise. The complexity of is_valid is in O ( $ | $ D $ | $ $ \times$ d $ ^2$ ). The complexity of clear is in O ( $ | $ D $ | $ $ \times$ d ). Other methods have all a constant time complexity. GenericMapItems

CGAL::Generic_map_min_items.name: CGAL::Generic_map_min_items
CGAL::Generic_map_min_items.brief: The class Generic_map_min_items defines void as the information associated with darts, and no attribute is enabled.
CGAL::Generic_map_min_items.detailed: Is model of GenericMapItems Example The following example shows the implementation of the Generic_map_min_items class. struct Generic_map_min_items { template < class CMap> struct Dart_wrapper {}; }; Combinatorial_map <d,Items,Alloc> Generalized_map<d,Items,Alloc>

CombinatorialMap.name: CombinatorialMap
CombinatorialMap.brief: The concept CombinatorialMap defines a d -dimensional combinatorial map.
CombinatorialMap.detailed: Refines GenericMap Has models CGAL::Combinatorial_map<d,Items,Alloc> For a combinatorial map, the function next is equal to $ \beta_1$ , previous is equal to $ \beta_0$ and the function opposite<i> is equal to $ \beta_i$ .

members.name: beta
members.brief: Returns $ \beta_j$ ( $ \beta_i$ ( d )).
members.detailed: Overloads of this member function are defined that take from one to nine integer as arguments. For each function, betas are applied in the same order as their indices are given as parameters. For example beta(d,1) = $ \beta_1$ ( d ), and beta(d,1,2,3,0) = $ \beta_0$ ( $ \beta_3$ ( $ \beta_2$ ( $ \beta_1$ ( d )))). 0 $ \leq$ i $ \leq$ dimension , 0 $ \leq$ j $ \leq$ dimension and d $ \in$ darts() .

members.name: beta
members.brief: Returns $ \beta_j$ ( $ \beta_i$ ( d )).
members.detailed: Overloads of this member function are defined that take from one to nine integer as arguments. 0 $ \leq$ i $ \leq$ dimension , 0 $ \leq$ j $ \leq$ dimension and d $ \in$ darts() .
members.argsstring: (Dart_const_descriptor d, int i, int j) const

members.name: beta
members.brief: Returns $ \beta_j$ ( $ \beta_i$ ( d )).
members.detailed: Overloads of this member function are defined that take from one to nine integer as template arguments. For each function, betas are applied in the same order as their indices are given as template arguments. For example beta <1>(d) = $ \beta_1$ ( d ), and beta <1,2,3,0>(d) = $ \beta_0$ ( $ \beta_3$ ( $ \beta_2$ ( $ \beta_1$ ( d )))). 0 $ \leq$ i $ \leq$ dimension , 0 $ \leq$ j $ \leq$ dimension and d $ \in$ darts() .

members.name: beta
members.brief: Returns $ \beta_j$ ( $ \beta_i$ ( d )).
members.detailed: Overloads of this member function are defined that take from one to nine integer as template arguments. 0 $ \leq$ i $ \leq$ dimension , 0 $ \leq$ j $ \leq$ dimension and d $ \in$ darts() .

members.name: opposite
members.brief: Returns a descriptor to a dart belonging to the same edge than dart d , and not to the same vertex.
members.detailed: null_descriptor if such a dart does not exist.

members.name: opposite
members.brief: Returns a const descriptor to a dart belonging to the same edge than dart d , and not to the same vertex, when the dart is const.
members.detailed: null_descriptor if such a dart does not exist.

members.name: is_valid
members.brief: Returns true iff the combinatorial map is valid.
members.detailed: A combinatorial map is valid (see Sections Mathematical Definitions and Combinatorial Map Properties ) if for all its darts d $ \in$ darts() : d is 0-free, or $ \beta_1(\beta_0(d))=d$ ; d is 1-free, or $ \beta_0(\beta_1(d))=d$ ; $ \forall$ i , 2 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension : d is i -free, or $ \beta_i(\beta_i(d))=d$ ; $ \forall$ i , $ \forall$ j , such that 0 $ \leq$ i $ \leq$ dimension -2 and i +2 $ \leq$ j $ \leq$ dimension : $ \beta_j(\beta_i(d))=\varnothing$ or ; $ \beta_j(\beta_i(\beta_j(\beta_i(d))))=d$ ; $ \forall$ i , 0 $ \leq$ i $ \leq$ dimension such that i -attributes are non void: $ \forall$ d2 in the same i -cell than d : d and d2 have the same i -attribute; $ \forall$ d2 in a different i -cell than d : d and d2 have different i -attributes.

members.name: link_beta
members.brief: Links d1 and d2 by $ \beta_i$ .
members.detailed: The combinatorial map can be no longer valid after this operation. If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==true , non void attributes of d1 and d2 are updated: if one dart has an attribute and the second dart not, the non null attribute is associated to the dart having a null attribute. If both darts have an attribute, the attribute of d1 is associated to d2 . 0 $ \leq$ i $ \leq$ dimension , d1 $ \in$ darts() , d2 $ \in$ darts() and ( i $ <$ 2 or d1 $ \neq$ d2 ).

members.name: unlink_beta
members.brief: Unlinks d and $ \beta_i$ ( d ) by $ \beta_i$ .
members.detailed: The combinatorial map can be no longer valid after this operation. Attributes of d and $ \beta_i$ ( d ) are not modified. 0 $ \leq$ i $ \leq$ dimension , d $ \in$ darts() , and d is not i -free.

members.name: is_sewable
members.brief: Returns true iff d1 can be i -sewn with d2 by keeping the generic map valid.
members.detailed: This is true if there is a bijection f between all the darts of the orbit D1 = $ \langle{}$ $ \beta_1$ , $ \ldots$ , $ \beta_{i-2}$ , $ \beta_{i+2}$ , $ \ldots$ , $ \beta_d$ $ \rangle{}$ ( d1 ) and D2 = $ \langle{}$ $ \beta_1$ , $ \ldots$ , $ \beta_{i-2}$ , $ \beta_{i+2}$ , $ \ldots$ , $ \beta_d$ $ \rangle{}$ ( d2 ) satisfying: f ( d1 )= d2 , $ \forall $ e $ \in$ D1 , $ \forall $ j $ \in$ {1, $ \ldots$ , i -2, i +2, $ \ldots$ , d }, f ( $ \beta_j$ ( e ))= $ \beta_j^{-1}$ ( f ( e )). 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension , d1 $ \in $ darts() , and d2 $ \in $ darts() .

members.name: sew
members.brief: i -sew darts d1 and d2 , by keeping the generic map valid.
members.detailed: Links by $ \beta_i $ two by two all the darts of the orbit D1 = $ \langle{}$ $ \beta_1$ , $ \ldots$ , $ \beta_{i-2}$ , $ \beta_{i+2}$ , $ \ldots$ , $ \beta_d$ $ \rangle{}$ ( d1 ) and D2 = $ \langle{}$ $ \beta_0$ , $ \beta_2$ , $ \ldots$ , $ \beta_{i-2}$ , $ \beta_{i+2}$ , $ \ldots$ , $ \beta_d$ $ \rangle{}$ ( d2 ) such that d2 = f ( d1 ), where f is the bijection between D1 and D2 satisfying: f ( d1 )= d2 , and for all e $ \in $ D1 , for all j $ \in $ {1, $ \ldots$ , i -2, i +2, $ \ldots$ , d }, f ( $ \beta_j$ ( e ))= $ \beta_j^{-1}$ ( f ( e )). If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==true , when necessary, non void attributes are updated to ensure the validity of the generic map: for each j -cells c1 and c2 which are merged into one j -cell during the sew, the two associated attributes attr1 and attr2 are considered. If one attribute is null_descriptor and the other not, the non null_descriptor attribute is associated to all the darts of the resulting cell. When the two attributes are non null_descriptor , functor Attribute_type<i>::type::On_merge is called on the two attributes attr1 and attr2 . If set, the dynamic onmerge function of i -attributes is also called on attr1 and attr2 . Then, the attribute attr1 is associated to all darts of the resulting j -cell. Finally, attribute attr2 is removed from the generic map. is_sewable<i> ( d1 , d2 ). Advanced If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==false , non void attributes are not updated; thus the generic map can be no more valid after this operation.

members.name: unsew
members.brief: i -unsew darts d and opposite <i>(d) , by keeping the generic map valid.
members.detailed: Unlinks by $ \beta_i$ all the darts in the orbit $ \langle{}$ $ \beta_1$ , $ \ldots$ , $ \beta_{i-2}$ , $ \beta_{i+2}$ , $ \ldots$ , $ \beta_d$ $ \rangle{}$ ( d ). If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==true , when necessary, non void attributes are updated to ensure the validity of the generic map: for each j -cell c split in two j -cells c1 and c2 by the operation, if c is associated to a j -attribute attr1 , then this attribute is duplicated into attr2 , and all the darts belonging to c2 are associated with this new attribute. Finally, the functor Attribute_type<i>::type::On_split is called on the two attributes attr1 and attr2 . If set, the dynamic onsplit function of i -attributes is also called on attr1 and attr2 . 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension , d $ \in $ darts() and d is not i -free. Advanced If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==false , non void attributes are not updated thus the generic map can be no more valid after this operation.

members.name: reverse_orientation
members.brief: Reverse the orientation (swap $ \beta_0$ and $ \beta_1$ links) of the entire map.

members.name: reverse_orientation_connected_component
members.brief: Reverse the orientation (swap $ \beta_0$ and $ \beta_1$ links) of the connected component containing the given dart.

members.name: null_dart_descriptor
members.brief: The null dart descriptor constant.
members.detailed: A dart d is i -free if beta(d, i)== null_dart_descriptor . Note that *null_dart_descriptor $ \notin$ darts() .

GenericMap.name: GenericMap
GenericMap.brief: The concept GenericMap defines a d -dimensional generic map. This concept is defined only to factorize the common notions between CombinatorialMap and GeneralizedMap concepts.
GenericMap.detailed: Refines DefaultConstructible A generic map has a set of darts D , and functions $ f_0$ , $ \ldots$ , $ f_{d}$ that link these darts between them. Has models CGAL::Combinatorial_map<d,Items,Alloc> CGAL::Generalized_map<d,Items,Alloc> CombinatorialMap GeneralizedMap

members.name: Dart
members.brief: Dart type.

members.name: Dart_descriptor
members.brief: Dart descriptor type.

members.name: Dart_const_descriptor
members.brief: Dart const descriptor type.
members.detailed: When using indices, Dart_const_descriptor is equal to Dart_descriptor .

members.name: Dart_info
members.brief: Information associated to each dart.

members.name: size_type
members.brief: Size type (an unsigned integral type).

members.name: Attributes
members.brief: The tuple of cell attributes.
members.detailed: It contains at most dimension +1 types (one for each possible cell of the generic map). Each type of the tuple must be either a model of the CellAttribute concept or void . The first type corresponds to 0-attributes, the second to 1-attributes and so on. If the i th type in the tuple is void , (i-1) -attributes are disabled. Otherwise, (i-1) -attributes are enabled and have the given type. If the size of the tuple is k , with k $< $ dimension +1 , $ \forall $ i : k $\leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension , i -attributes are disabled.

members.name: Attribute_type
members.brief: Attribute_type <i>::type is the type of i -attributes, a model of CellAttribute concept.
members.detailed: Attribute_type<i>::type::Dart_descriptor is equal to Dart_descriptor , and Attribute_type<i>::type::Dart_const_descriptor is equal to Dart_const_descriptor . 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension and i -attributes are non void . It can be implemented using a nested template class.

members.name: Attribute_descriptor
members.brief: Attribute_descriptor <i>::type is a descriptor to i -attributes.
members.detailed: 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension and i -attributes are non void. It can be implemented using a nested template class.

members.name: Attribute_const_descriptor
members.brief: Attribute_descriptor <i>::type is a const descriptor to i -attributes.
members.detailed: 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension and i -attributes are non void. It can be implemented using a nested template class.

members.name: Dart_range
members.brief: Range of all the darts of the generic map.
members.detailed: This type is a model of Range concept, its iterator type is bidirectional and its value type is Dart .

members.name: Dart_const_range
members.brief: Const range of all the darts of the generic map.
members.detailed: This type is a model of ConstRange concept, its iterator type is bidirectional and its value type is Dart .

members.name: Attribute_range
members.brief: Attribute_range <i>::type is the range of all the i -attributes.
members.detailed: Attribute_range <i>::type is a model of Range concept, its iterator type is bidirectional and its value type is Attribute_type<i>::type . 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension and i -attributes are non void. It can be implemented using a nested template class.

members.name: Attribute_const_range
members.brief: Attribute_const_range <i>::type is the const range of all the i -attributes.
members.detailed: Attribute_const_range <i>::type is a model of ConstRange concept, its iterator type is bidirectional and its value type is Attribute_type<i>::type . 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension and i -attributes are non void. It can be implemented using a nested template class.

members.name: Dart_of_orbit_range
members.brief: Range of all the darts of the <I...> orbit.
members.detailed: This type is a model of Range concept, its iterator type is forward and its value type is Dart .

members.name: Dart_of_orbit_const_range
members.brief: Const range of all the darts of the <I...> orbit.
members.detailed: This type is a model of ConstRange concept, its iterator type is forward and its value type is Dart .

members.name: Dart_of_cell_range
members.brief: Range of all the darts of an i -cell.
members.detailed: Cells are considered in dim dimension, with 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dim+1 and 0 $ \leq $ dim $ \leq $ dimension . If i==dim+1 , range of all the darts of a connected component. This type is a model of Range concept, its iterator type is forward and its value type is Dart .

members.name: Dart_of_cell_const_range
members.brief: Const range of all the darts of the i -cell.
members.detailed: Cells are considered in dim dimension, with 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dim+1 and 0 $ \leq $ dim $ \leq $ dimension . If i==dim+1 , range of all the darts of a connected component. This type is a model of ConstRange concept, its iterator type is forward and its value type is Dart .

members.name: One_dart_per_incident_cell_range
members.brief: Range of one dart of each i -cell incident to one j -cell.
members.detailed: Cells are considered in dim dimension, with 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dim+1 , 0 $ \leq $ j $ \leq $ dim+1 and 0 $ \leq $ dim $ \leq $ dimension . If i == dim+1 , consider each connected component instead of each i -cell. If j == dim+1 , consider one connected component instead of one j -cell. This type is a model of Range concept, its iterator type is forward and its value type is Dart .

members.name: One_dart_per_incident_cell_const_range
members.brief: Const range of one dart of each i -cell incident to one j -cell.
members.detailed: Cells are considered in dim dimension, with 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dim+1 , 0 $ \leq $ j $ \leq $ dim+1 and 0 $ \leq $ dim $ \leq $ dimension . If i==dim+1 , consider each connected component instead of each i -cell. If j==dim+1 , consider one connected component instead of one j -cell. This type is a model of ConstRange concept, its iterator type is forward and its value type is Dart .

members.name: One_dart_per_cell_range
members.brief: Range of one dart of each i -cell of the generic map.
members.detailed: Cells are considered in dim dimension, with 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dim+1 and 0 $ \leq $ dim $ \leq $ dimension . If i==dim+1 , consider each connected component instead of each i -cell. This type is a model of Range concept, its iterator type is forward and its value type is Dart .

members.name: One_dart_per_cell_const_range
members.brief: Const range of one dart of each i -cell of the generic map.
members.detailed: Cells are considered in dim dimension, with 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dim+1 and 0 $ \leq $ dim $ \leq $ dimension . If i==dim+1 , consider each connected component instead of each i -cell. This type is a model of ConstRange concept, its iterator type is forward and its value type is Dart .

members.name: dimension
members.brief: The dimension d of the generic map.

members.name: NB_MARKS
members.brief: The number of available Boolean marks of the generic map.

members.name: null_descriptor
members.brief: A null descriptor.
members.detailed: This descriptor is not valid and should not be used to access a dart or an attribute.

members.name: GenericMap
members.brief: Construct a new generic map from another one.
members.detailed: The new generic map is created by copying the darts and the non void attributes of bmap. Map must be a model of GenericMap concept, which can be defined with a different dimension and/or different attributes than *this . In this case, only dimensions that are common to bmap and *this , and only non void i-attributes of bmap whose info type is the same to the info of non void i-attributes of *this , are copied.

members.name: is_empty
members.brief: Returns true iff the generic map is empty, i.e. it contains no dart.

members.name: is_without_boundary
members.brief: Returns true iff the generic map is without i -boundary.
members.detailed: The map is without i -boundary if there is no i -free dart. 1 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension .

members.name: is_without_boundary
members.brief: Returns true iff the generic map is without boundary in all dimensions.

members.name: number_of_darts
members.brief: Returns the number of darts in the generic map.

members.name: number_of_attributes
members.brief: Returns the number of i -attributes in the generic map.
members.detailed: 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension , and i -attributes are non void.

members.name: upper_bound_on_dart_ids
members.brief: Returns an upper bound of the id of dart descriptors if indices are used or 0 otherwise.

members.name: upper_bound_on_attribute_ids
members.brief: Returns an upper bound of the id of i -attributes descriptors if indices are used or 0 otherwise.

members.name: is_dart_used
members.brief: Returns true if d is a descriptor of a used dart (i.e. valid).

members.name: is_free
members.brief: Returns true iff dart d is i -free.
members.detailed: 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension .

members.name: is_free
members.brief: Returns true iff dart d is i -free.
members.detailed: 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension .

members.name: highest_nonfree_dimension
members.brief: Returns the highest dimension i such that dart d is not i -free.
members.detailed: -1 if d is free for any dimension.

members.name: other_extremity
members.brief: Returns a descriptor to a dart belonging to the other vertex of the edge containing dart d (but not necessarily to the same edge).
members.detailed: null_descriptor if such a dart does not exist.

members.name: other_extremity
members.brief: Returns a const descriptor to a dart belonging to the other vertex of the edge containing dart d , when the dart is const (but not necessarily to the same edge).
members.detailed: null_descriptor if such a dart does not exist.

members.name: next
members.brief: Returns a descriptor to a dart belonging to the next edge after d , that does not belong to the same 0 -cell than d and that belongs to the same i -cell than d for each i , 2 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension .

members.name: next
members.brief: Returns a const descriptor to a dart belonging to the next edge after d , that does not belong to the same 0 -cell than d and that belongs to the same i -cell than d for each i , 2 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension .

members.name: previous
members.brief: Returns a descriptor to a dart belonging to the previous edge before d , that does not belong to the same 0 -cell than d and that belongs to the same i -cell than d for each i , 2 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension .

members.name: previous
members.brief: Returns a const descriptor to a dart belonging to the previous edge before d , that does not belong to the same 0 -cell than d and that belongs to the same i -cell than d for each i , 2 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension .

members.name: opposite
members.brief: Returns a descriptor to a dart belonging to the opposite i -cell than d .
members.detailed: This dart does not belong to the same 0 -cell than d , nor to the same i -cell, but belongs to the same i -cell than d for each j , 2 $ \leq $ j $ \leq $ dimension , j $ \neq $ i . 2 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension .

members.name: opposite
members.brief: Returns a const descriptor to a dart belonging to the opposite i -cell than d .
members.detailed: This dart does not belong to the same 0 -cell than d , nor to the same i -cell, but belongs to the same i -cell than d for each j , 2 $ \leq $ j $ \leq $ dimension , j $ \neq $ i . 2 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension .

members.name: display_characteristics
members.brief: Displays on os the number of elements of the generic map.
members.detailed: Its number of darts, its number of i -cells, for each i , 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension , and its number of connected components. Example of output for a 3D combinatorial map containing two disjoint combinatorial tetrahedra: #Darts=24, #0-cells=8, #1-cells=12, #2-cells=8, #3-cells=2, #ccs=2

members.brief: Returns a descriptor to the i -attribute associated to dart d .
members.detailed: 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension , and i -attributes are non void .

members.brief: Returns the information of the const i -attribute *ah .

members.brief: A shortcut for info_of_attribute<i> ( attribute<i> (adart)) for const descriptor.

members.brief: A shortcut for dart_of_attribute<i> ( attribute<i> (adart)) for const descriptor.

members.name: is_attribute_used
members.brief: Returns true if ah points to a used i-attribute (i.e. valid).
members.detailed: 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension , and i -attributes are non void .

members.name: dart_descriptor
members.brief: Returns the dart descriptors of d .
members.detailed: d $ \in $ darts() .

members.name: dart_descriptor
members.brief: Returns the dart const descriptors of d .
members.detailed: d $ \in $ darts() .

members.name: darts
members.brief: Returns a range of all the darts in the generic map.

members.name: darts
members.brief: Returns a const range of all the darts in the generic map.

members.name: attributes
members.brief: Returns a range of all the i -attributes in the generic map.
members.detailed: 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension , and i -attributes are non void.

members.name: attributes
members.brief: Returns a const range of all the i -attributes in the generic map.
members.detailed: 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension , and i -attributes are non void.

members.name: darts_of_orbit
members.brief: Returns a range of all the darts of the orbit <I...>(d) .
members.detailed: The first element in the range points onto d . d $ \in $ darts() and I... is a sequence of integers $ i_1$ , $ \ldots$ , $ i_k$ , such that 0 $ \leq $ $ i_1$ $ < $ $ i_2$ $ < $ $ \ldots$ $ < $ $ i_k$ $ \leq $ dimension .

members.name: darts_of_orbit
members.brief: Returns a const range of all the darts of the orbit <I...>(d) .
members.detailed: The first element in the range points onto d . Same as for the non const version.

members.name: darts_of_cell
members.brief: Returns a range of all the darts of the i -cell containing d .
members.detailed: The first element in the range points onto d . i -cells are considered in dim dimension. If i==dim+1 , range of all the darts of the connected component containing d . d $ \in $ darts() , 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dim+1 and 0 $ \leq $ dim $ \leq $ dimension .

members.name: darts_of_cell
members.brief: Returns a const range of all the darts of the i -cell containing d .
members.detailed: The first element in the range points onto d . i -cells are considered in dim dimension. If i==dim+1 , const range of all the darts of the connected component containing d . Same as for the non const version.

members.name: one_dart_per_incident_cell
members.brief: Returns a range of one dart of each i -cell incident to the j -cell containing d .
members.detailed: The first element in the range points onto d . Cells are considered in dim dimension. If i==dim+1 , consider each connected component instead of each i -cell. If j==dim+1 , consider the connected component containing d instead of the j -cell. d $ \in $ darts() , 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dim+1 , 0 $ \leq $ j $ \leq $ dim+1 and 0 $ \leq $ dim $ \leq $ dimension .

members.name: one_dart_per_incident_cell
members.brief: Returns a const range of one dart of each i -cell incident to the j -cell containing d .
members.detailed: The first element in the range points onto d . Cells are considered in dim dimension. If i==dim+1 , consider each connected component instead of each i -cell. If j==dim+1 , consider the connected component containing d instead of the j -cell. Same as for the non const version.

members.name: one_dart_per_cell
members.brief: Returns a range of one dart of each i -cell in the generic map.
members.detailed: Cells are considered in dim dimension. If i==dim+1 , range of one dart of each connected component in the generic map. 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dim+1 and 0 $ \leq $ dim $ \leq $ dimension .

members.name: one_dart_per_cell
members.brief: Returns a const range of one dart of each i -cell in the generic map.
members.detailed: Cells are considered in dim dimension. If i==dim+1 , const range of one dart of each connected component in the generic map. Same as for the non const version.

members.name: create_dart
members.brief: Creates a new dart in the generic map, and returns the corresponding descriptor.
members.detailed: Calls the constructor of dart having T1 as parameter. A new dart is initialized to be i -free, $ \forall $ i : 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension , and to have no associated attribute for each non void attribute. Overloads of this member function are defined that take from zero to nine arguments. With zero argument, create_dart() creates a new dart by using the default constructor.

members.name: erase_dart
members.brief: Removes d from the generic map.
members.detailed: d $ \in $ darts() .

members.name: create_attribute
members.brief: Creates a new i -attribute in the generic map, and returns the corresponding descriptor.
members.detailed: Calls the constructor of i -attribute having T1 as parameter. Overloads of this member function are defined that take from zero to nine arguments. With zero argument, create_attribute <i>() creates a new i -attribute by using the default constructor. 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension , and i -attributes are non void.

members.name: erase_attribute
members.brief: Removes the i -attribute *ah from the generic map.
members.detailed: 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension , i -attributes are non void, and *ah $ \in $ attributes<i>() .

members.name: set_attribute
members.brief: Associates the i -attribute of all the darts of the i -cell containing d to a .
members.detailed: d $ \in $ darts() , 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension , i -attributes are non void, and *ah $ \in $ attributes<i>() .

members.name: clear
members.brief: Deletes all the darts and all the attributes of the generic map.

members.name: operator=
members.brief: Assignment operator.
members.detailed: All darts and attributes are duplicated, and the former generic map is deleted.

members.name: swap
members.brief: Swap the current generic map with bmap .
members.detailed: There is no copy of darts and attributes thus this method runs in constant time.

members.name: are_attributes_automatically_managed
members.brief: Returns the status of the management of the attributes of the generic map.
members.detailed: true if the high level operations update the non void attributes (default value); false otherwise.

members.name: set_automatic_attributes_management
members.brief: Set the status of the management of the attributes of the generic map.
members.detailed: Advanced After calling set_automatic_attributes_management(false) , all high level operations will not update non void attributes, until the call of set_automatic_attributes_management(true) . The call of set_automatic_attributes_management(true) call the correct_invalid_attributes() function.

members.name: correct_invalid_attributes
members.brief: Correct the invalid attributes of the generic map.
members.detailed: We can have invalid attribute either if we have called set_automatic_attributes_management(false) before to use some modification operations or if we have modified the generic map by using low level operations. $ \forall i $ , 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension such that the i-attributes are non void, $ \forall $ d $ \in $ darts() : if there exists a dart d2 in the same i-cell than d with a different i-attribute, then the i-attribute of d2 is set to the i-attribute of d ; if there exists a dart d2 in a different i-cell than d with the same i-attribute, then the i-attribute of all the darts in i-cell( d ) is set to a new i-attribute (copy of the original attribute); ensures that dart_of_attribute(d) $ \in $ i-cell( d ).

members.name: onsplit_function
members.brief: Return the current dynamic on-split function associated with i-attributes.
members.detailed: This is a boost::function returning void and having two references to Attribute_type<i>::type as parameters. The on-split function is returned by reference so that we can modify it.
members.return_type: boost::function< void(typename Attribute_type < i >::type &, typename Attribute_type < i >::type &)> &
members.definition: boost::function< void(typename Attribute_type< i >::type &, typename Attribute_type< i >::type &)> & GenericMap::onsplit_function

members.name: onsplit_function
members.brief: Return the current dynamic on-split function associated with i-attributes, when *this is const.

members.return_type: const boost::function< void(typename Attribute_type < i >::type &, typename Attribute_type < i >::type &)> &
members.definition: const boost::function< void(typename Attribute_type< i >::type &, typename Attribute_type< i >::type &)> & GenericMap::onsplit_function

members.name: onmerge_function
members.brief: Return the current dynamic on-merge function associated with i-attributes.
members.detailed: This is a boost::function returning void and having two references to Attribute_type<i>::type as parameters. The on-merge function is returned by reference so that we can modify it.

members.name: get_new_mark
members.brief: Reserves a new mark.
members.detailed: Returns its index. If there is no more available free mark, throw the exception Exception_no_more_available_mark.

members.name: is_reserved
members.brief: Returns true iff m is a reserved mark of the generic map.
members.detailed: 0 $ \leq $ m $ < $ NB_MARKS .

members.name: is_marked
members.brief: Returns true iff dart d is marked for m .
members.detailed: is_reserved(m) and d $ \in $ darts() .

members.name: mark
members.brief: Marks dart d for m .
members.detailed: is_reserved ( m ) and d $ \in $ darts() .

members.name: unmark
members.brief: Unmarks dart d for the mark m .
members.detailed: is_reserved ( m ) and d $ \in $ darts() .

members.name: negate_mark
members.brief: Inverse the mark m for all the darts of the generic map.
members.detailed: All the marked darts become unmarked and all the unmarked darts become marked. is_reserved ( m ).

members.name: unmark_all
members.brief: Unmarks all the darts of the generic map for m .
members.detailed: is_reserved ( m ).

members.name: number_of_marked_darts
members.brief: Returns the number of marked darts for m .
members.detailed: is_reserved ( m ).

members.name: number_of_unmarked_darts
members.brief: Return the number of unmarked darts for m .
members.detailed: is_reserved ( m ).

members.name: free_mark
members.brief: Frees mark m .
members.detailed: is_reserved ( m ).

members.name: make_combinatorial_hexahedron
members.brief: Creates a combinatorial hexahedron (six combinatorial quadrangles 2-sewn together), and adds it in the generic map.
members.detailed: Returns a descriptor on one dart of this combinatorial hexahedron. dimension $\geq$ 2. make_edge() make_combinatorial_polygon() make_combinatorial_tetrahedron()

members.name: make_combinatorial_polygon
members.brief: Creates a combinatorial polygon of length lg (a cycle of lg edges), and adds it in the generic map.
members.detailed: Returns a descriptor on one dart of this combinatorial polygon. dimension $ \geq$ 1 and lg $ >$ 0. make_edge() make_combinatorial_tetrahedron() make_combinatorial_hexahedron()

members.name: make_combinatorial_tetrahedron
members.brief: Creates a combinatorial tetrahedron (four combinatorial triangles 2-sewn together), and adds it in the generic map.
members.detailed: Returns a descriptor on one dart of this combinatorial tetrahedron. dimension $ \geq$ 2. make_edge() make_combinatorial_polygon() make_combinatorial_hexahedron()

members.name: make_edge
members.brief: Creates an isolated edge (two darts sewn to represent one edge and two vertices) and adds it in the generic map.
members.detailed: Returns a descriptor on one dart of this edge. dimension $ \geq$ 2. make_combinatorial_polygon() make_combinatorial_tetrahedron() make_combinatorial_hexahedron()

members.name: insert_cell_0_in_cell_1
members.brief: Inserts a 0-cell in the 1-cell containing d .
members.detailed: Returns next(d) , a descriptor on one dart belonging to the new 0-cell. dimension $ \geq$ 1 and d $ \in $ darts() . See examples for combinatorial map in fig__fig_cmap_insert_vertex and for generalized map in fig__fig_gmap_insert_vertex. If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==true , if 1-attributes are non void , Attribute_type<1>::type::On_split ( a , a' ) is called, with a the original 1-attribute associated with d and a' the new 1-attribute created during the operation. If set, the dynamic on-split function of 1-attributes is also called on a and a' . Advanced If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==false , non void attributes are not updated; thus the generic map can be no more valid after this operation. insert_cell_0_in_cell_2() insert_cell_1_in_cell_2() insert_cell_1_between_two_cells_2() insert_dangling_cell_1_in_cell_2() insert_cell_2_in_cell_3 <InputIterator>() remove_cell <i>()

members.name: insert_cell_0_in_cell_2
members.brief: Inserts a 0-cell in the 2-cell containing d .
members.detailed: The 2-cell is split in triangles, one for each initial edge of the facet. Returns a descriptor on one dart belonging to the new 0-cell. dimension $ \geq$ 2 and d $ \in $ darts() . See examples for combinatorial map in fig__fig_cmap_triangulation and for generalized map in fig__fig_gmap_triangulation. If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==true , if 2-attributes are non void , Attribute_type<2>::type::On_split ( a , a' ) is called, with a the original 2-attribute associated with d and a' each new 2-attribute created during the operation. If set, the dynamic on-split function of 2-attributes is also called on a and a' . Advanced If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==false , non void attributes are not updated; thus the generic map can be no more valid after this operation. insert_cell_0_in_cell_2() insert_cell_1_in_cell_2() insert_cell_1_between_two_cells_2() insert_dangling_cell_1_in_cell_2() insert_cell_2_in_cell_3 <InputIterator>() remove_cell <i>()

members.name: insert_cell_1_in_cell_2
members.brief: Inserts a 1-cell in the 2-cell containing d1 and d2 .
members.detailed: Returns previous(d1) , a descriptor on one dart belonging to the new 1-cell. is_insertable_cell_1_in_cell_2 ( d1 , d2 ). See examples for combinatorial map in fig__fig_cmap_insert_edge and for generalized map in fig__fig_gmap_insert_edge. If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==true , if 2-attributes are non void , Attribute_type<2>::type::On_split ( a , a' ) is called, with a the original 2-attribute associated with d1 and a' the new 2-attribute created during the operation. If set, the dynamic on-split function of 2-attributes is also called on a and a' . Advanced If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==false , non void attributes are not updated; thus the generic map can be no more valid after this operation. is_insertable_cell_1_in_cell_2() insert_cell_0_in_cell_1() insert_cell_0_in_cell_2() insert_cell_1_between_two_cells_2() insert_dangling_cell_1_in_cell_2() insert_cell_2_in_cell_3 <InputIterator>() remove_cell <i>()

members.name: insert_cell_1_between_two_cells_2
members.brief: Inserts a 1-cell between the 2-cell containing d1 and the one containing d2 .
members.detailed: Returns previous(d1) , a descriptor on one dart belonging to the new 1-cell. is_insertable_cell_1_between_two_cells_2 ( d1 , d2 ). If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==true , call Attribute_type<i>::type::On_merge ( a , a' ) is called for all enabled i-attributes, for i>=2, with a the original 2-attribute associated with d1 and a' the original 2-attribute associated with d2 . If set, the dynamic on-merge function of i-attributes is also called on a and a' . Advanced If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==false , non void attributes are not updated; thus the generic map can be no more valid after this operation. is_insertable_cell_1_between_two_cells_2() insert_cell_0_in_cell_1() insert_cell_0_in_cell_2() insert_cell_1_in_cell_2() insert_dangling_cell_1_in_cell_2() insert_cell_2_in_cell_3 <InputIterator>() remove_cell <i>()

members.name: insert_cell_1
members.brief: Call insert_cell_1_in_cell_2() if is_insertable_cell_1_in_cell_2(d1, d2) , otherwise call insert_cell_1_between_two_cells_2() .
members.detailed: insert_cell_1_in_cell_2() insert_cell_1_between_two_cells_2() is_insertable_cell_1_in_cell_2()

members.name: insert_cell_2_in_cell_3
members.brief: Inserts a 2-cell along the path of 1-cells containing darts given by the range [afirst,alast) .
members.detailed: Returns opposite <2>(*afirst) , a descriptor on one dart belonging to the new 2-cell. is_insertable_cell_2_in_cell_3 ( afirst , alast ). See examples for combinatorial map in fig__fig_cmap_insert_facet and for generalized map in fig__fig_gmap_insert_facet. If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==true , if 3-attributes are non void , Attribute_type<3>::type::On_split ( a , a' ) is called, with a the original 3-attribute associated with d and a' the new 3-attribute created during the operation. If set, the dynamic on-split function of 3-attributes is also called on a and a' . Advanced If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==false , non void attributes are not updated; thus the generic map can be no more valid after this operation. is_insertable_cell_2_in_cell_3 <InputIterator>() insert_cell_0_in_cell_1() insert_cell_0_in_cell_2() insert_cell_1_in_cell_2() insert_cell_1_between_two_cells_2() insert_dangling_cell_1_in_cell_2() remove_cell <i>()

members.name: insert_dangling_cell_1_in_cell_2
members.brief: Inserts a 1-cell in a the 2-cell containing d , the 1-cell being attached only by one of its extremity to the 0-cell containing d .
members.detailed: Returns previous(d) , a descriptor on the dart belonging to the new 1-cell and to the new 0-cell. dimension $ \geq$ 2 and d $ \in $ darts() . See examples for combinatorial map in fig__fig_cmap_insert_edge and for generalized map in fig__fig_gmap_insert_edge. Advanced If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==false , non void attributes are not updated; thus the generic map can be no more valid after this operation. insert_cell_0_in_cell_1() insert_cell_0_in_cell_2() insert_cell_1_in_cell_2() insert_cell_1_between_two_cells_2() insert_cell_2_in_cell_3 <InputIterator>() remove_cell <i>()

members.name: is_insertable_cell_1_in_cell_2
members.brief: Returns true iff it is possible to insert a 1-cell in the generic map between d1 and d2 .
members.detailed: This is possible if d1 $ \neq $ d2 and d1 can be reached from d2 by using some previous and next calls. dimension $ \geq$ 2, d1 $ \in $ darts() , and d2 $ \in $ darts() . insert_cell_1_in_cell_2() is_insertable_cell_2_in_cell_3 <InputIterator>()

members.name: is_insertable_cell_1_between_two_cells_2
members.brief: Returns true iff it is possible to insert a 1-cell in the generic map between d1 and d2 .
members.detailed: This is possible if d1 $ \neq $ d2 and d1 can not be reached from d2 by using some previous and next calls. dimension $ \geq$ 2, d1 $ \in $ darts() , and d2 $ \in $ darts() . insert_cell_1_between_two_cells_2()

members.name: is_insertable_cell_2_in_cell_3
members.brief: Returns true iff it is possible to insert a 2-cell in the generic map along the path of darts given by the range [afirst,alast) .
members.detailed: The 2-cell can be inserted iff the ordered list of darts form a closed path of edges inside a same volume. dimension $ \geq$ 3. insert_cell_2_in_cell_3 <InputIterator>() is_insertable_cell_1_in_cell_2()

members.name: is_removable
members.brief: Returns true iff the i -cell containing d can be removed.
members.detailed: An i -cell can be removed if i == dimension or if i == dimension -1 or if i $ < $ dimension -1 and the i -cell containing d is incident to at most two ( i+1 )-cells. 0 $ \leq $ i $ \leq $ dimension and d $ \in $ darts() . remove_cell <i>()

members.name: remove_cell
members.brief: Removes the i -cell containing d .
members.detailed: Returns the number of darts removed from the generic map. is_removable<i> ( d ). See examples in fig__fig_cmap_insert_vertex , fig__fig_cmap_insert_edge and fig__fig_cmap_insert_facet . If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==true , if i $ < $ dimension , and i+1 -attributes are non void , and if there are two distinct ( i+1 )-cells around dart d , Attribute_type<i+1>::type::On_merge ( a1 , a2 ) is called, with a1 the ( i+1 )-attribute associated to d , and a2 the ( i+1 )-attribute associated to $ \beta_{i+1}$ ( d ). If set, the dynamic on-merge function of i+1 -attributes is also called on a1 and a2 . If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==true , if a j -cell is disconnected in two j -cells during the operation, and if j -attributes are non void, Attribute_type<j>::type::On_split ( a , a' ) is called with a the original j -attribute and a' the new j -attribute created due to the disconnection. If set, the dynamic on-split function of j -attributes is also called on a and a' . Advanced If are_attributes_automatically_managed() ==false , non void attributes are not updated; thus the generic map can be no more valid after this operation. is_removable <i>() insert_cell_0_in_cell_1() insert_cell_0_in_cell_2() insert_cell_1_in_cell_2() insert_cell_1_between_two_cells_2() insert_dangling_cell_1_in_cell_2() insert_cell_2_in_cell_3 <InputIterator>()

GenericMapItems.name: GenericMapItems
GenericMapItems.brief: The concept GenericMapItems allows to customize a d D generic map by choosing the information associated with darts, by enabling and disabling some attributes, and by choosing to use indices or handles. For that, it defines an inner class template named Dart_wrapper , with one template parameter, Map , a model of the GenericMap concept. This inner class can define the two types Dart_info and Attributes .
GenericMapItems.detailed: Has models CGAL::Generic_map_min_items GenericMap Example The following examples show two possible models of the GenericMapItems concept: the first one for a generic map without dart information, nor enabled attributes, the second one for a generic map that uses std::uint16_t as indices, with a double associated with each dart, and edge attributes enabled, and associated with a Cell_attribute containing an int . struct Example_Item_1 { template < class CMap > struct Dart_wrapper {}; }; struct Example_Item_2 { typedef CGAL::Tag_true Use_index; // CGAL::Tag_false by default typedef std::uint16_t Index_type; // std::uint32_t by default template < class GMap > struct Dart_wrapper { typedef double Dart_info; typedef CGAL::Cell_attribute<GMap, int> Edge_attrib; typedef std::tuple<void,Edge_attrib> Attributes; }; };

members.name: Use_index
members.brief: If Use_index is CGAL::Tag_true , uses indices as descriptors.
members.detailed: Otherwise (if this type is not defined or different from CGAL::Tag_true ), use handles.

members.name: Index_type
members.brief: Number type used for indices when Use_index is CGAL::Tag_true.
members.detailed: By default, use std::uint32_t.

members.name: Dart_wrapper
members.brief: Wrapper class defining type of information associated with darts and types of attributes.
members.detailed: The class Dart_wrapper<Map> must provide: Dart_wrapper<Map>::Dart_info , the type of information associated with darts. Not defined or equal to void to have no information. Dart_wrapper<Map>::Attributes The tuple of attributes, containing at most Map::dimension+1 types (one for each possible cell of the generic map). Each type of the tuple must be either a model of the CellAttribute concept or void . The first type corresponds to 0-attributes, the second to 1-attributes and so on. If the $ i^{\mbox{th}}$ type in the tuple is void , ( i -1)-attributes are disabled. Otherwise, ( i -1)-attributes are enabled and have the given type. If the size of the tuple is k , with k < Map::dimension +1, $ \forall$ i : k $ \leq$ i $ \leq$ Map::dimension , i -attributes are disabled. If this type is not defined, all attributes are disabled. It can be implemented using a nested template class.

PkgCombinatorialMapsRef.name: PkgCombinatorialMapsRef
PkgCombinatorialMapsRef.detailed: Guillaume Damiand This package implements Combinatorial Maps in d dimensions. A combinatorial map is a data structure enabling to represent an orientable subdivided object by describing all the cells of the subdivision (for example in 3D vertices, edges, faces, volumes) and all the incidence and adjacency relationships between these cells. Information can be associated to cells thanks to attributes. In 2D, a combinatorial map is equivalent to a halfedge data structure. The package provides basic creation, modification operations, and several iterators enabling to run through some specific part of the object. Introduced in: CGAL 3.9 BibTeX: cgal:d-cm-26a License: LGPL Classified Reference Pages Concepts GenericMap GenericMapItems CellAttribute CombinatorialMap Classes CGAL::Combinatorial_map <d,Items,Alloc> CGAL::Cell_attribute <CMap,Info_,Tag,OnMerge,OnSplit> CGAL::Cell_attribute_with_id <CMap,Info_,Tag,OnMerge,OnSplit> CGAL::Generic_map_min_items IO Functions for CMap std::ostream& operator<< (std::ostream& os, const GenericMap& amap) std::ifstream& operator>> (std::ifstream& is, GenericMap& amap)

members.name: operator<<
members.brief: Writes amap in os , using our own internal file format in XML.
members.detailed: Writes both the topology of the combinatorial map and its enabled attributes.

members.name: operator>>
members.brief: Reads amap from is , using our own internal file format in XML.
members.detailed: Reads both the topology of the combinatorial map and its enabled attributes which are present in is . Note that if amap is not empty before the reading, the new map is added in the previous one.
